dc_table.5: Trivial — almost anyone can do this
dc_table.10: Easy — a competent person succeeds most of the time
dc_table.20: Hard — even skilled characters may fail

conditions.Poisoned: Disadvantage on attack rolls and ability checks
conditions.Stunned: Incapacitated, can't move, auto-fail STR/DEX saves, attacks have advantage
conditions.Blinded: Auto-fail sight checks, attacks have disadvantage, attackers have advantage
conditions.Frightened: Disadvantage on checks/attacks while source is in line of sight, can't willingly move closer
conditions.Grappled: Speed becomes 0, ends if grappler incapacitated or forced apart
conditions.Prone: Disadvantage on attacks, melee attacks against have advantage, ranged have disadvantage
conditions.Restrained: Speed 0, attacks have disadvantage, DEX saves have disadvantage, attackers have advantage
conditions.Paralyzed: Incapacitated, auto-fail STR/DEX saves, attacks have advantage, melee crits within 5ft
conditions.Invisible: Impossible to see without magic/special sense, attacks have advantage, attackers have disadvantage
conditions.Charmed: Can't attack charmer, charmer has advantage on social checks
conditions.Exhaustion: Cumulative: 1=disadvantage on checks, 2=speed halved, 3=disadvantage on attacks/saves, 4=HP max halved, 5=speed 0, 6=death
conditions.Concentration: Broken by: taking damage (DC 10 or half damage, whichever higher), incapacitated, or casting another concentration spell

action_economy.Action: Attack, Cast Spell, Dash, Disengage, Dodge, Help, Hide, Ready, Search, Use Object
action_economy.Bonus Action: Off-hand attack, some spells, class features (Cunning Action, etc.)
action_economy.Reaction: Opportunity attack, Shield spell, Counterspell, Readied action
action_economy.Free: Drop item, speak briefly, interact with one object
action_economy.Movement: Move up to speed, can split before/after actions

cover.Half Cover: +2 AC, +2 DEX saves (low wall, furniture, creature)
